note: Jātaka samodhāna; nāga ca supaṇṇo ca = nāga vs garuḍa as past identities, naga-being in a list with supaṇṇa

claim: gloss: aṇḍaja = the egg-born nāga; classes nāga among egg-born beings
note: lexical gloss aṇḍajena = aṇḍakose nibbattena nāgena
kuñjarāti nāgaṃ ālapati - addresses the elephant; war/city-storming scene
elephant with soṇḍā (trunk) tearing pillars and gates; war elephant
nāgo nāvāya vaṇṇena - elephant in shape of a boat carrying the layman; simile vehicle
gloss of preceding verse; nāgo nāvāya vaṇṇena = boat-shaped elephant
bandhanāgāra = prison (bandhana+agāra); not the nāga morpheme
yakkha-nāga-balikamma = offerings to yakkhas and nāgas; nāga-being in cultic list
nāgabalo = elephant-strength epithet of a monkey bodhisatta; simile-compound, not a nāga referent
nāgamissati = na+āgamissati (will not come); morphological false friend
battle taunt 'ehi nāga nivattassu' - addressing a warrior/bull as nāga; martial vocative
gloss of preceding battle verse; nāga as martial hero/bull vocative
nāgabalo of a deer (migarājā); elephant-strength simile compound
bandhanāgāre = prison; bandhana+agāra
bandhanāgārāni = prisons; bandhana+agāra
nānāgandhapupphādi = various-scented-flowers; nānā+gandha, not nāga

claim: a fierce nāga seized a boat on the Ganges; nāga has power over river/men
note: luddena nāgena ... gahītanāvaṃ on the Ganges - water nāga seizing a vessel
bandhanāgāre = prison; bandhana+agāra

claim: supaṇṇa-king seizes a nāga-king by the tail; garuḍa preys on nāgas
note: nāgarāja vs supaṇṇa predation; nāga uses coils (bhoga) round the banyan
pānāgāresu = drinking-houses (pāna+agāra); nāgarehi = townsfolk/citizens
nāgarā = citizens went to the king; nāgabalo = robber of elephant-strength (simile)
nāgabalā gaṇikā = courtesan of elephant-strength; simile compound
nāgabalo bhatiko = hireling of elephant-strength; simile compound
nāgabalo of a brahmin youth; elephant-strength simile compound
nāgamiṃsu = na+āgamiṃsu (did not come); cf nāgatā nearby = na+āgatā
mātaṅgaraññeva nāgo - elephant in the forest like a king; Khaggavisāṇa-style solitary-elephant verse
appossukko mātaṅgaraññeva nāgo - solitary forest elephant simile
explicit gloss: mātaṅgoti hatthī vuccati, nāgoti mahantādhivacanametaṃ; sīlavahatthināga, Pālileyyaka
nāgabalo of King Kāliṅga; elephant-strength simile compound for a man
nāgarā = citizens petition the king; nāgabalo = robber of elephant-strength (simile)
nāgā ca supaṇṇā ca bhūmaṭṭhakā devā - nāgas in a list of beings attending a festival
nāgamāsi = na+āgamāsi (did not come back); puna nagaraṃ nāgamāsi

claim: a fiercely-venomous nāga keeping virtue is not killed; venom + uposatha/sīla
note: ghoraviso nāgo sīlavā - venomous serpent observing virtue; Saṅkhapāla/Bhūridatta-type sīla theme
jackal slain by an elephant (nāgena hato); the Sīhacamma/jackal-and-elephant verse
gloss: jambuko nāgena hato - jackal killed by the elephant
bandhanāgāra = prison; bandhana+agāra
bandhanāgāre = prison; bandhana+agāra
bandhanāgāre = prison; bandhana+agāra
nāgamissati = na+āgamissati (will not come to me); byasanaṃ na āgamissati
nāgamissati = na+āgamissati; explicit gloss byasanaṃ nāgacchati = will not come
crow, quail, frog, fly killed the elephant (nāgaṃ aghātesuṃ) - Latukika Jātaka enmity verse
bandhanāgāre = prison; bandhana+agāra
Mātuposaka(-nāga) Jātaka frame; tassa nāgassa vippavāsena - the elephant's absence; mother-supporting tusker
verse: the elephant's absence makes trees flower; Mātuposaka tusker
'gaṇhāhi nāga kabaḷaṃ' - take the morsel, elephant; feeding the captive tusker
mahānāga of the blind mother (andhā apariṇāyikā); Mātuposaka tusker, not a human
'muñcathetaṃ mahānāgaṃ' release this great elephant who supports his mother; Mātuposaka
gloss: the elephant says he supports his blind mother; Mātuposaka tusker
mutto bandhanā nāgo ... kuñjaro - the elephant freed from bondage; Mātuposaka
the elephant freed from bondage taught the king the ten royal duties; Mātuposaka tusker
samodhāna: mātuposaka-nāga = bodhisatta; hatthinī mother, hatthācariya; mother-supporting tusker
vasanāgāre = dwelling-house (vasana+agāra); not nāga
nāgamuṇḍā = proper name of a slave-woman (Nāgamuṇḍā dāsī); human name built on nāga-
gloss lists nāga-yakkha-supaṇṇa-sakka as classes of beings being asked 'who are you?'; nāga among beings
bandhanāgārāni = prisons; bandhana+agāra
gilānāgilāne = sick-and-not-sick (gilāna+agilāna); not nāga

claim: a hooded nāga received/coiled him with its coils; hood (phaṇa) + coils (bhoga)
note: nāgo jātaphaṇo ... paccaggahi maṃ bhogehi - hood-raised serpent with coils
'Ānando paṇḍito nāgo' - Ānanda the wise nāga; honorific of a human in samodhāna verse
'kiṃ te nāgo vā supaṇṇo vā devo vā ... deti' - does a nāga/garuḍa/deva give you; nāga among supernatural givers
dassanaṃ panāgamma = pana+āgamma (on coming to sight); not nāga
Kāliṅgabodhi: the mounted nāga could not pass over the bodhimaṇḍa; royal riding-elephant
king went near the Bodhi on his mighty nāga (elephant); Kāliṅgabodhi
nāgā ca ... kuñjarā - elephants and tuskers cannot approach this spot; Kāliṅgabodhi
abhijāto nāgo ... kuñjaraṃ dantiṃ - send the noble tusker; Kāliṅgabodhi elephant
the king urged the nāga (elephant) forward; Kāliṅgabodhi
the nāga trumpeted like a heron and shrank back unable to bear the load; riding-elephant
gloss: uposatha-kula elephants, hatthiratana; eight elephant families; cakkavatti's elephant-jewel
'tava nāgo niruddho, aññaṃ hatthiṃ saṅkamā' - your elephant has died, mount another; explicit hatthi
nāgaṃ khīṇāyukaṃ ... aññaṃ saṅkama nāgaṃ - the elephant is dying, mount another; riding-elephant
gloss: nāgo khīṇāyuko ... añño nāgo uposathakulato āgantvā piṭṭhiṃ upanāmesi - elephant
the king mounted another nāga (elephant) which fell dead on the spot; Kāliṅgabodhi
nāgabalo migo = deer of elephant-strength; simile compound
'Pālileyyo tadā nāgo' - Pālileyyaka the elephant in samodhāna; the elephant who served the Buddha
nāgabalo thāmasampanno of a deer (sīlavā mentioned, captured stag); elephant-strength simile compound

claim: a nāga-possessed banyan tree (nāgapariggahita); nāgas inhabit/own trees by water
note: nāgapariggahitaṃ nigrodharukkhaṃ - banyan guarded/possessed by a nāga

claim: by the nāga's supernatural power tents and bedding poured forth for the merchants
note: nāgānubhāvena ... pagghariṃsu - nāga's iddhi providing goods (Valāhassa/serpent-merchant theme)

claim: nāgas came forth armed - 25 nāgas, 300 archers etc.; nāgas as an armed host
note: tato nāgā nikkhamiṃsu sannaddhā - serpent-beings emerging armed (nāga-army)

claim: the nāgas returned to their own nāga-realm (nāgabhavana)
note: attano nāgabhavanameva gatā - serpent-beings return to the nāga-world
'nāgo chetvāva bandhanaṃ' - like an elephant breaking its bonds; renunciation simile (cf Dhp elephant)
bandhanāgāre = (figurative) prison of the womb; bandhana+agāra
bandhanāgāre = prison; bandhana+agāra
'kacchaṃ nāgānaṃ bandhatha' - gird the elephants' harness, fasten the breastplate; royal war-elephants
'nāgo yathā paṅkamajjhe byasanno' - like an elephant stuck in mud; explicitly an elephant-in-mire simile (gloss confirms kāmapaṅka)
gloss of preceding: nāgo yathā ... an elephant sunk in mud, simile for being mired in sensuality
'nāge dehi ... hemakappanavāsase' - give an elephant with gold trappings; gift-list with chariots/horses
nānāgottā = of various clans (nānā+gotta); not nāga
nāgamissati = na+āgamissati (will not come/be again); puna na bhavissati nāgamissati

claim: wise men of old gave up nāga-glory (nāgasampatti) to keep the uposatha
note: nāgasampattiṃ pahāya uposathavāsaṃ vasiṃsu - Campeyya frame; renouncing nāga-state for uposatha

claim: Campeyya nāga-king ruled a nāga-realm in the river Campā; nāga-world under the water
note: Campeyya Jātaka opening; nāgabhavana in the river, nāgarājā ruling, jewelled pavilion underwater

claim: the nāga-king, weary of the animal birth, kept uposatha to escape to the human realm and win the path
note: 'kiṃ me imāya tiracchānayoniyā' - nāga as animal birth; keeps uposatha; nāga-maidens break his vow

claim: the nāga-king refuses to eat frogs/parched-corn lest he kill, even at risk of death in the box
note: snake-charmer feeds the captured Campeyya nāga; nāga's restraint over diet

claim: folk call it uraga and nāga (serpent); the snake-charmer captured it
note: 'uragoti cāhu nāgoti naṃ āhu janā' - explicitly serpent (uraga=nāga); Campeyya captured

claim: a nāga-maiden is asked how the powerful nāga fell into a beggar's hand; nāga-strength + nāgakaññā
note: nāgakaññe ... gahītanāgaṃ - nāga-maiden and captured Campeyya nāga

claim: the nāga could reduce a city to ashes yet honours Dhamma and practises austerity
note: 'nagarampi nāgo bhasmaṃ kareyya ... dhammaṃ apacāyamāno' - venom/destructive power + restraint; Campeyya

claim: gloss: not a poor nāga - he has 16000 women and lives in a water-chamber (vārigeha)
note: daliddanāgo ... vārigehasayā = nāga sleeping in a water-dwelling; Campeyya's wealth
'mutto campeyyako nāgo' - the freed Campeyya nāga addresses the king; named serpent-king (passing speech frame)

claim: 'a man should not trust a non-human (amānusa)'; the nāga is classed as amānusa
note: 'yaṃ mānuso vissase amānusamhi ... dakkhemu te nāga nivesanāni' - nāga as non-human whose dwelling will be seen

claim: repeat: nāga as amānusa (non-human) whose abode the king will visit
note: same Campeyya verse repeated; nāga = amānusa

claim: may the supaṇṇas shun your whole nāga-clan; garuḍa-nāga enmity
note: 'sabbañca te nāgakulaṃ supaṇṇā ... vivajjayantu' - nāga-clan and its predator garuḍa; Campeyya boon
king pities the nāga-clan; 'I will render service to your nāga-clan' - passing address to Campeyya's kin
'dakkhemu nāgassa nivesanāni' - let us see the nāga's dwelling; nāge here = harness-elephants but nāgassa = serpent Campeyya
nānāgandhasamīritaṃ = wafted with various scents (nānā+gandha); not nāga

claim: gloss: the adorned nāga-realm (nāgabhavana), Campeyya nāga-king's mansion thronged by nāga-maidens
note: alaṅkatanāgabhavana, campeyyanāgarāja, nāgakaññāgaṇa - serpent-realm description

claim: 'no such splendour exists in the human world - why, nāga, do you do austerities?'; nāga-realm surpasses men
note: kiṃ patthayaṃ nāga tapo karosi - king to Campeyya about why a nāga with such glory keeps uposatha

claim: refrain: nothing like this nāga-splendour in the human world; why does the nāga practise austerity
note: Campeyya nāga-mansion praise refrain; nāga keeps tapo despite nāga-luxury

claim: refrain: rivers/fish unlike the human world; why does the nāga do austerities
note: Campeyya refrain; nāga-realm vs human world

claim: refrain: birds unlike the human world; why does the nāga do austerities
note: Campeyya refrain; nāga-realm splendour

claim: refrain: trees/fruits unlike the human world; why does the nāga do austerities
note: Campeyya refrain; nāga-realm

claim: refrain: divine scents from the lotus-ponds; why, nāga, do you do austerities
note: Campeyya refrain; nāga-realm splendour vs human world
'having seen the women and you, nāga, I will do much merit' - king to Campeyya; passing address

claim: the Campeyya nāga-king gave up nāga-glory to keep the uposatha (samodhāna)
note: campeyyanāgarājā = bodhisatta; 'nāgasampattiṃ pahāya uposathavāsaṃ vasiṃsu'
jhānāgāre = meditation-chamber (jhāna+agāra); not nāga

nāgavittodā glossed below as nāgānaṃ vittikara udaka (waters that delight nāgas) - here a compound of rivers; treated as river/water description, gloss-target ambiguous; coded nonlexical-leaning but see p049

claim: gloss: nāgavittodā = waters endowed with the wealth/delight of nāgas; rivers associated with nāgas
note: nāgavittodāti nāgānaṃ vittikārena ... udakena samannāgatā - nāgas linked to waters
'paṇḍaranāgavaro' - Paṇḍaraka the excellent nāga, in an uddāna list of Jātaka names; named serpent-king
Chaddanta opening: hatthināgā, soṇḍā (trunk), dantā (tusks), 8000 elephants; nāgarājā = the elephant-king Chaddanta
Chaddanta nāgarājā with 8000 elephants in the golden cave; bull-elephant king
Chaddanta nāgarājā strikes a sāl tree with his kumbha (frontal globe); young elephants (taruṇanāga); bull-elephant
Chaddanta nāgarājā bathes; young elephants use trunks (soṇḍā) to bathe him; bull-elephant
queen wants the elephant's twin tusks (yamakadante); Chaddanta the bull-elephant
kuñjaraṃ chabbisāṇaṃ ... aṭṭhasahassanāgā, īsādantā - six-tusked elephant guarded by 8000 elephants; Chaddanta
gloss: īsādantā = tusks like chariot-poles; 8000 elephants guard the elephant-king; Chaddanta
the elephant-king bathes in Chaddanta lake, garlanded, surrounded by 8000 elephants; Chaddanta bull
six-tusked elephant guarded by 8000 elephants; Chaddanta (repeat verse)
seeing the elephant's gait at bathing; the hunter's pit; Chaddanta bull-elephant
the hunter shot the elephant (nāgaṃ) with a broad arrow from his pit; Chaddanta
the wounded elephant trumpeted, all the elephants roared; Chaddanta herd
gloss: the 8000 elephants heard the trumpet and roared in fear; Chaddanta herd
gloss: 8000 elephants scatter; the nāgarājā (Chaddanta) strikes the ground with his foot-nail; bull-elephant
the arrow-pierced elephant, unangered, speaks to the hunter; Chaddanta
gloss: she saw in a dream such an elephant; wants the elephant's six-coloured tusks; Chaddanta
the elephant tells the hunter to cut his tusks before he dies; Chaddanta
gloss: 'the elephant is slain by me, take his tusks'; Chaddanta
gloss: before the (other) elephants came he died; Chaddanta herd
the elephants found no enemy and returned; Chaddanta herd
terrified by the elephant-slaying (nāgavadha), the elephants return to the elephant-king; gajapaccamitta = elephant-foe; Chaddanta
prov said citizen but window is Chaddanta funeral: 8000 elephants, young elephants lift the elephant-king's body with tusks; clearly elephant
the elephants wept, threw dust on their heads, went home led by queen Sabbabhadda; Chaddanta herd
'nāgo hato handa imassa dantā' - the elephant is slain, here are his tusks; gajuttamassa = best of elephants; Chaddanta
the hunter presents the tusks: 'that elephant was slain by me'; Chaddanta

claim: Paṇḍara nāga-king; garuḍa-king asks how to seize nāgas since many garuḍas die catching them
note: Paṇḍara Jātaka opening; nāgarāja Paṇḍara vs supaṇṇa, the secret of catching nāgas

claim: as a garuḍa seizes the Paṇḍaraka nāga, so fear pursues the indiscreet; nāga as garuḍa's prey
note: 'nāgaṃ yathā paṇḍarakaṃ supaṇṇo' - garuḍa seizing the Paṇḍara serpent (simile but serpent referent + predation)

claim: repeat: fear follows the babbler as a garuḍa the Paṇḍaraka nāga
note: same Paṇḍara simile; serpent prey of garuḍa

claim: gloss: as a garuḍa overtook me the Paṇḍaraka nāga; serpent caught by garuḍa
note: 'yathā maṃ paṇḍarakanāgaṃ supaṇṇo anvāgato' - Paṇḍara serpent's own words; garuḍa predation
gloss addresses Paṇḍaraka nāga (dujivha = forked-tongue); āsīvisa simile nearby; serpent named, but passing address (lexical gloss)

claim: the Paṇḍara nāga, sensing a garuḍa, made a huge body, raised hood (phaṇa) and coils (bhoga), swallowed stones
note: nāgabhavana entry; nāgo māpeti attabhāvaṃ, phaṇa + bhoga + naṅguṭṭha; serpent shapeshift/defence vs garuḍa
two pure puññakhandha beings 'like draught-horses, nāgā' approach the ascetic; nāga as praise-epithet of noble men (cf next purisanāgā)
gloss: assavāhāva nāgā = purisanāgā (men-nāgas) like two yoked draught-horses; honorific of noble men
anāgamma = na+āgamma (without coming to/relying on); bahussutaṃ anāgamma
anāgamma = na+āgamma; explicit gloss bahussutaṃ anāgamma taṃ asaddahanto

claim: hierarchy of beings: devas, yakkhas, gandhabbas, nāgas, venateyyas, kinnaras stand in ranked order
note: gandhabbānaṃ antare nāgā, nāgānaṃ antare venateyyā - nāgas placed in the cosmic order of beings

claim: wise men of old gave up great nāga-glory to keep the uposatha
note: nāgasampattiṃ pahāya uposathavāsaṃ upavasiṃsu - Bhūridatta/Saṅkhapāla-type uposatha frame

claim: 'where is this huge-bodied one taken? what will you do with the nāga?' - the captured nāga is to be eaten
note: bhīmakāyo nāga ... nīyati bhojanatthā (next rows); Bhūridatta-type serpent captured for food

claim: 'this nāga, a huge swollen-bodied serpent (uraga), is led off for food; its flesh is sweet'
note: 'nāgo ayaṃ ... pavaḍḍhakāyo urago mahanto' - explicitly serpent (uraga); eaten for flesh; Bhūridatta

claim: 'if this huge serpent (uraga) is led off for food, I give 16 oxen - release the nāga'
note: 'pavaḍḍhakāyo urago mahanto ... nāgaṃ imaṃ muñcatha' - serpent (uraga) ransomed; Bhūridatta
